claim: Explorer and cross-checker evidence is usable for current working-tree source and test-definition facts, and they correctly keep unexecuted tests, external provider sandbox enforcement, and working-tree/uncommitted status out of verified facts. The evidence is not sufficient to claim committed-release behavior, executed test success, or externally enforced read-only execution.

evidence.summary: requiredEvidenceRoles returns explorer, cross-checker, and verifier for cross_check; evidence results with missing data, clarify/block recommendations, blocked/failed verdicts, or failed statuses become blockers.
evidence.why: Confirms the workflow requires this verifier result and preserves unresolved missing evidence as blockers.

evidence.summary: Technical-risk terms including field, schema, api, auth, state, data flow, migration, database and related terms route demand handling to careful with cross_check evidence policy.
evidence.why: Confirms the brownfield/schema/API/data-risk routing claim used by previous evidence results.

evidence.summary: PRD readiness collects missing slots, active blockers, open questions, unresolved assumptions, incomplete evidence agents, evidence result issues, and agreement conflicts; prd_ready is true only when blockers are empty.
evidence.why: Confirms assumptions and missing evidence are not promoted to PRD-ready facts.

evidence.summary: Evidence task planning adds explorer, cross-checker for cross_check, and verifier; evidence agreement emits EVIDENCE_AGENT_CONFLICT when recommendations disagree in blocking ways.
evidence.why: Confirms all required roles are planned and conflicts remain explicit blockers.

evidence.summary: Dispatch plan declares dry_run default, explicit execute requirements, no business-code writes, read_only agent instruction, and prompt hard rules requiring read-only JSON evidence with assumptions separated from facts.
evidence.why: Confirms the dispatch/prompt evidence cited by explorer and cross-checker is present and usable.

evidence.summary: Execution config sets codex_sandbox read-only and codex_approval never for codex provider, custom_sandbox read-only for custom provider, and runtime returns before provider execution unless executeAgents and allowAgentDispatch are both true.
evidence.why: Confirms default dry-run and explicit authorization gates, while limiting the verified fact to local configuration.

evidence.summary: Runtime prompts planned evidence agents, parses JSON output into agent_results, then re-runs inspectDemandPrdReadiness with those evidence_results.
evidence.why: Confirms evidence agent outputs are integrated into readiness evaluation.

evidence.summary: CLI workflow options default executeAgents and allowAgentDispatch to false and only enable them via explicit flags.
evidence.why: Confirms CLI authorization defaults cited in previous evidence.

evidence.summary: Demand dispatch/evidence CLI command calls runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime with executeAgents and allowAgentDispatch and exits 1 on blocked result.
evidence.why: Confirms CLI wiring to dispatch runtime.

evidence.summary: Test definition asserts an existing inventory API schema field demand routes careful, uses cross_check, and needs explorer/cross-checker/verifier.
evidence.why: Confirms previous evidence accurately describes test-definition coverage, not executed test results.

evidence.summary: Test definitions cover required evidence-agent completion, existing blockers, evidence conflicts, missing evidence, and clarify recommendations blocking PRD readiness.
evidence.why: Confirms blocker-preservation behavior is represented in tests.

evidence.summary: Test definitions cover dispatch planning, dry-run behavior, execute authorization, executing all three evidence roles, codex read-only config, and conflict blocking.
evidence.why: Confirms previous test evidence is present as source-level test definitions.

evidence.summary: Read-only git status reports src/demand/router.ts, src/demand/evidence-dispatch.ts, __tests__/demand-router.test.ts, and __tests__/demand-evidence-dispatch.test.ts as untracked, and src/cli/yolo.ts as modified; git emitted sandbox cache warnings.
evidence.why: Confirms evidence describes the current working tree rather than a committed release state.

risks: Treating this as committed-release behavior would be incorrect because key files are untracked or modified in the current working tree.
Treating test definitions as passing tests would be incorrect because tests were not executed under the read-only protocol.
Treating provider sandboxing as externally enforced would be incorrect because only local config and prompt instructions were verified.
missing: Actual test execution/pass status remains unverified.
External provider enforcement of read-only sandbox settings remains unverified beyond local configuration and tests.
Committed or shipped release state remains unverified because relevant files are untracked or modified.

result.notes: Evidence paths and summaries from explorer and cross-checker are usable for current working-tree source/test-definition facts, and their missing/risk sections correctly avoid promoting assumptions. PRD readiness should remain blocked for executed-test status, external sandbox enforcement, and committed-release claims unless those are explicitly out of scope.
